# 1 Kings 19

## Verses (21)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | Ahab gave Jezebel news of all Elijah had done, and how he had put all the prophets to death with the sword. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | Then Jezebel sent a servant to Elijah, saying, May the gods' punishment be on me if I do not make your life like the life of one of them by tomorrow about this time. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | And he got up, fearing for his life, and went in flight, and came to Beer-sheba in Judah, parting there from his servant; | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | While he himself went a day's journey into the waste land, and took a seat under a broom-plant, desiring for himself only death; for he said, It is enough: now, O Lord, take away my life, for I am no better than my fathers. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | And stretching himself on the earth, he went to sleep under the broom-plant; but an angel, touching him, said to him, Get up and have some food. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | And looking up, he saw by his head a cake cooked on the stones and a bottle of water. So he took food and drink and went to sleep again. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | And the angel of the Lord came again a second time, and touching him said, Get up and have some food, or the journey will be overmuch for your strength. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | So he got up and took food and drink, and in the strength of that food he went on for forty days and nights, to Horeb, the mountain of God. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | And there he went into a hole in the rock for the night; then the word of the Lord came to him, saying, What are you doing here, Elijah? | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | And he said, I have been burning for the honour of the Lord, the God of armies; for the children of Israel have not kept your agreement; they have made destruction of your altars, and have put your prophets to death with the sword: till I, even I, am the only one living; and now they are attempting to take away my life. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | Then he said, Go out and take your place on the mountain before the Lord. Then the Lord went by, and mountains were parted by the force of a great wind, and rocks were broken before the Lord; but the Lord was not in the wind. And after the wind there was an earth-shock, but the Lord was not in the earth-shock. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | And after the earth-shock a fire, but the Lord was not in the fire. And after the fire, the sound of a soft breath. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | And Elijah, hearing it, went out, covering his face with his robe, and took his place in the opening of the hole. And there a voice came to him saying, What are you doing here, Elijah? | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | And he said, I have been burning for the honour of the Lord, the God of armies; for the children of Israel have not kept your agreement; they have had your altars broken down, and have put your prophets to death with the sword: till I, even I, am the only one living; and now they are attempting to take away my life. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | And the Lord said to him, Go back on your way through the waste land to Damascus; and when you come there, put the holy oil on Hazael to make him king over Aram; | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | And on Jehu, son of Nimshi, making him king over Israel; and on Elisha, the son of Shaphat of Abel-meholah, to be prophet in your place. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | And it will come about that the man who gets away safe from the sword of Hazael, Jehu will put to death; and whoever gets away safe from the sword of Jehu, Elisha will put to death. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | But I will keep safe seven thousand in Israel, all those whose knees have not been bent to Baal, and whose mouths have given him no kisses. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | So he went away from there and came across Elisha, the son of Shaphat, ploughing with twelve yoke of oxen, he himself walking with the twelfth; and Elijah went up to him and put his robe on him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | And letting the oxen be where they were, he came running after Elijah, and said, Only let me give a kiss to my father and mother, and then I will come after you. But he said to him, Go back again; for what have I done to you? | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | And he went back, and took the oxen and put them to death, and cooking their flesh with the yokes of the oxen, he gave the people a feast. Then he got up and went after Elijah and became his servant. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
